name: Northwind Traders — Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (SAP ECC Replacement)
scope_summary: Full D365 Finance & Operations implementation replacing SAP ECC across Finance, Supply Chain, and Warehouse Management. 18-month program in 3 phases. Includes full data migration from SAP, integration with 4 external systems, and 250-user training program. All delivered by Microsoft Professional Services.

profile.ai_recommendation_text: The Northwind Traders D365 F&O engagement presents strong financial merit — a 32% margin on a $1.6M deal is best-in-class and the SOW quality, estimation accuracy, and compliance scores collectively signal sound pre-sales diligence. Strategic alignment to the Dynamics pillar is solid, and the 86% delivery success rate on comparable programs provides reasonable execution confidence for a Tier 3 approval. However, the risk profile score of 5.5 is a material concern for a high-complexity SAP ECC replacement, where data migration fidelity, cutover planning, and organizational change management are historically the primary failure modes. The Azure ACR of $14K/month (6% of deal) is below expected consumption attachment for a workload of this scale and should be structurally addressed before contract execution.
ai_conditions: A formal Risk Mitigation Plan must be approved by the assigned Delivery Principal before contract signature, explicitly covering data migration strategy, cutover sequencing, and change management governance for the SAP ECC transition.

An Azure consumption attachment roadmap is required — identify and scope qualifying workloads (e.g., Azure Integration Services, Azure Monitor, Power BI Premium) to bring ACR toward a 10%+ deal-value threshold over the engagement lifecycle.

Confirm Microsoft vendor co-investment or FastTrack for Dynamics 365 participation; absence of any vendor contribution on a high-complexity, Tier 3 deal increases delivery risk and reduces Microsoft alignment support.

Validate executive sponsorship and a formal steering committee structure at Northwind Traders prior to execution — C-level commitment is a leading indicator of success on ERP replacement programs of this scope.

score_dimensions: Alignment to company priority pillars (Cloud, AI, Security, Dynamics, Data)
Inverse risk: solution complexity, deal size, vendor dependency
Scope completeness, exclusions, assumptions, acceptance criteria
Historical success rate on comparable engagements
Confidence in effort estimates based on complexity and comparables
Regulatory and compliance overhead (HIPAA, GDPR, SOX, FFIEC)
Reuse of playbooks, SOW templates, and delivery accelerators
Partner/subcontractor % — impact on margin and delivery control
Monthly ACR generated — strategic value to Microsoft ecosystem
